goal: Fix PR description creation so that newlines are preserved correctly when passed to `gh pr create --body`, by replacing the broken `shQuote` implementation (which uses JSON.stringify and produces literal \n) with POSIX single-quote escaping.

requirements.text: Replace the `shQuote` function in `extensions/worktree.ts` (around line 695-696) so it uses POSIX single-quote escaping (`'...'` with embedded single quotes escaped as `'\''`) instead of `JSON.stringify()`. This ensures real newlines and special characters are preserved correctly in shell arguments.
The new `shQuote` implementation should follow the same POSIX single-quoting pattern already used in `inline-git-commands.ts` in this repo.
requirements.source: Root Cause section — 'inline-git-commands.ts has the correct implementation using POSIX single-quoting'

requirements.text: Update the `extractPrBody` test(s) to handle single-quoted bodies instead of JSON-stringified bodies, and remove the broken `.commands` expectation.
All existing call sites of `shQuote` (17+ usages) must continue to work correctly with the new implementation — the fix must be a drop-in replacement.
requirements.source: Key Change #1 — 'strictly more correct for all 17+ call sites'

requirements.text: (Optional) Improve the AI prompt structure in `getGeneratedTexts` in `extensions/worktree.ts` for better PR description quality.

checks.text: Verify that `shQuote` applied to a string containing real newlines (e.g., `'line1\nline2'`) produces a shell-safe quoted string that, when evaluated by bash, preserves the actual newline characters.
checks.source: Root Cause — JSON.stringify converts real newlines to literal \n

checks.text: Verify that `shQuote` correctly handles strings containing single quotes by escaping them as `'\''`.
Verify that `shQuote` handles edge cases: empty strings, strings with only special characters, strings with backslashes, strings with double quotes.
checks.source: REQ-4 — drop-in replacement for all call sites

checks.text: Verify that `extractPrBody` tests pass after being updated to expect single-quoted output format.
Run the full test suite (`npm test` or equivalent) to confirm no regressions across all 17+ `shQuote` call sites.

ambiguities.text: Two overlapping test files exist for PR creation. The plan suggests the plan-generated one (`currently-when-...`) has better infrastructure but does not definitively resolve which should be canonical or whether both should be updated.
Should `--body-file` be used as a fallback mechanism if edge cases appear with the `shQuote` fix, or is the `shQuote` fix considered fully sufficient?

evidence.reason: Contains the broken `shQuote` function (line ~695) and `getGeneratedTexts` — the primary files to modify.
Contains the correct POSIX single-quote escaping implementation to use as a reference pattern.
Contains test files including `extractPrBody` tests that need updating.

outOfScope: Switching to `--body-file` approach (only a fallback if shQuote fix proves insufficient)
Refactoring or consolidating the two overlapping test files beyond what is needed for this fix
Changes to GitHub API interaction beyond shell argument quoting
advisoryNotes: The reference implementation in `inline-git-commands.ts` should be consulted to ensure consistency with the existing repo pattern.
Consider extracting the POSIX `shQuote` into a shared utility to avoid having two implementations in the codebase.
If any of the 17+ call sites pass arguments with unusual characters (e.g., Unicode, null bytes), those should be spot-checked after the change.
The `--body-file` approach remains a viable fallback if any edge cases surface with very long PR descriptions or unusual character combinations.
